threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'going for a pint' means a casual evening; locals know it's a serious commitment, especially with the pub closing times around here.
Last night on the 59, a lad was singing his own rap while the driver tried to keep a straight face. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing football in the street, while an older man sold fresh pretzels from his bicycle nearby. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a U-Bahn station that doubles as a club on weekends—get your techno fix without leaving the tracks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their patience at the BART delays. They quietly calculate their next move instead of panicking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 38 this morning, clearly late to a wedding. He stood out among the usual morning hustle like a misplaced wedding cake.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think 'Gràcia' is just about Gaudí. They miss the hidden art squats and the locals in the squares, sipping vermut and sharing stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their reluctance to speak Catalan, even when everyone around is using it like it’s second nature.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'banger' instead of a 'sausage roll'—the locals will spot that in a heartbeat.
Saw a guy on the 35 last night talking to an empty seat like it was his best mate. Brixton after dark never disappoints.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a kid ride their bike past a guy selling homemade hummus out of a cardboard box—nothing says Neukölln like that mashup.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people casually sipping coffee in parks while their dogs run free, ignoring the leash laws like it's a form of public art.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they casually reference the ‘BART Meltdown’ like it’s just another Tuesday. The rest just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy jamming on a ukulele while the whole bus was stuck in traffic. Felt like a mini concert right there in the Tenderloin. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think paella is our thing, but on the daily, it's all about el plat de llenties and a good coca recomenda from the local forns.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still look for the escalators at Diagonal; locals know the stairs are quicker after years of dodging tourists.
